Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

{
  "file_path": "/private/var/folders/k0/jp_x85zj55nf3ssm76z3dmmh0000gn/T/llm-bench-_0dg34p2/generate_service.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments.

Usage:
  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service
"""

import argparse
import sys

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Generate systemd service unit files')

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument('--name', required=True, help='Service name')
    parser.add_argument('--exec', required=True, help='Executable path')

    # Optional arguments with defaults
    parser.add_argument('--user', default='root', help='User to run as (default: root)')
    parser.add_argument('--description', default=None, help='Service description (default: "{name} service")')
    parser.add_argument('--after', default=['network.target'], nargs='+', help='Services to start after (default: network.target)')
    parser.add_argument('--restart', default='on-failure',
                       choices=['no', 'on-success', 'on-failure', 'on-abnormal', 'on-watchdog', 'on-abort', 'always'],
                       help='Restart policy (default: on-failure)')
    parser.add_argument('--restart-sec', type=int, default=5, help='Restart delay in seconds (default: 5)')
    parser.add_argument('--output', default=None, help='Output file name (default: {name}.service)')
    parser.add_argument('--wanted-by', default='multi-user.target', help='Target that wants this service (default: multi-user.target)')
    parser.add_argument('--env', action='append', help='Environment variables (KEY=VALUE)')
    parser.add_argument('--working-dir', help='Working directory')

    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Set default description if not provided
    if args.description is None:
        args.description = f'{args.name} service'

    # Set default output filename if not provided
    if args.output is None:
        args.output = f'{args.name}.service'

    # Generate service file content
    service_content = f"""[Unit]
Description={args.description}
After={' '.join(args.after)}

[Service]
Type=simple
User={args.user}
ExecStart={args.exec}
Restart={args.restart}
RestartSec={args.restart_sec}
"""

    # Add environment variables if provided
    if args.env:
        for env_var in args.env:
            service_content += f"Environment={env_var}\n"

    # Add working directory if provided
    if args.working_dir:
        service_content += f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}\n"

    # Add install section
    service_content += f"""

[Install]
WantedBy={args.wanted_by}
"""

    # Write to output file
    try:
        with open(args.output, 'w') as f:
            f.write(service_content)
        print(f"Successfully generated {args.output}")
        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error writing to {args.output}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.exit(main())
"
}

files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments.

Usage:
  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service
"""

import argparse
import sys

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Generate systemd service unit files')

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument('--name', required=True, help='Service name')
    parser.add_argument('--exec', required=True, help='Executable path')

    # Optional arguments with defaults
    parser.add_argument('--user', default='root', help='User to run as (default: root)')
    parser.add_argument('--description', default=None, help='Service description (default: "{name} service")')
    parser.add_argument('--after', default=['network.target'], nargs='+', help='Services to start after (default: network.target)')
    parser.add_argument('--restart', default='on-failure',
                       choices=['no', 'on-success', 'on-failure', 'on-abnormal', 'on-watchdog', 'on-abort', 'always'],
                       help='Restart policy (default: on-failure)')
    parser.add_argument('--restart-sec', type=int, default=5, help='Restart delay in seconds (default: 5)')
    parser.add_argument('--output', default=None, help='Output file name (default: {name}.service)')
    parser.add_argument('--wanted-by', default='multi-user.target', help='Target that wants this service (default: multi-user.target)')
    parser.add_argument('--env', action='append', help='Environment variables (KEY=VALUE)')
    parser.add_argument('--working-dir', help='Working directory')

    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Set default description if not provided
    if args.description is None:
        args.description = f'{args.name} service'

    # Set default output filename if not provided
    if args.output is None:
        args.output = f'{args.name}.service'

    # Generate service file content
    service_content = f"""[Unit]
Description={args.description}
After={' '.join(args.after)}

[Service]
Type=simple
User={args.user}
ExecStart={args.exec}
Restart={args.restart}
RestartSec={args.restart_sec}
"""

    # Add environment variables if provided
    if args.env:
        for env_var in args.env:
            service_content += f"Environment={env_var}\n"

    # Add working directory if provided
    if args.working_dir:
        service_content += f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}\n"

    # Add install section
    service_content += f"""

[Install]
WantedBy={args.wanted_by}
"""

    # Write to output file
    try:
        with open(args.output, 'w') as f:
            f.write(service_content)
        print(f"Successfully generated {args.output}")
        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error writing to {args.output}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.exit(main())

[Unit]
Description=Web Application Server
After=network.target systemd-networkd-wait-online.service

[Service]
Type=simple
User=nginx
ExecStart=/opt/webapp/server.py
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
Environment=NODE_ENV=production
Environment=PORT=8080
WorkingDirectory=/opt/webapp


[Install]
WantedBy=graphical.target
